mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative or flat 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0 percent over a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls signals broad-based economic expansion, increasing aggregate demand for corporate goods and services, which lifts equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero directional accuracy below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test, indicating the interaction does not predict returns.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, industrial production exceeding 103.0 indicates supply chain bottlenecks and capacity constraints that compress profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral in more than 60% of triggered instances during late-cycle periods, falsifying the margin compression channel.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP directly increases corporate cash flows, which equity markets price in immediately, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter without requiring specific threshold crossings.
failure_condition: Rank IC of quarterly GDP growth against 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPIAUCSL exceeding 0.3 percent triggers market repricing of future monetary tightening, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances following CPIAUCSL MoM spikes above 0.3 percent over a 4-year test window.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which sustains consumer demand and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The interaction signal yields a directional accuracy below 48% over a 3-year walk-forward test, proving the joint labor metrics do not predict short-term equity moves.

mechanism_narrative: During credit expansion phases, rising residential investment coupled with elevated consumer credit balances fuel demand-driven earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter when RSAFS exceeds 450 billion dollars.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative or flat in more than 60% of triggered instances during credit expansion regimes, falsifying the demand channel.
